prompt:
Set up a LexiBridge Learning Center Operations Suite that enables an on-prem English training business to run content publishing, bookings, staff leave approvals, and cashflow tracking from a single browser-based portal. Administrators manage users and roles, while Content Editors create and maintain English knowledge items (vocabulary, phrases, grammar points, example sentences, phonetics, images, and audio) with draft/publish and publish-unpublish controls; the Thymeleaf UI should provide side-by-side editing and preview, plus bulk import/export via CSV/Excel/JSON uploads with inline deduplication prompts when a term matches an existing normalized spelling plus phonetic. Moderators use an inbox to review posts, comments, and Q&A entries containing rich text and media, with clear status cues (pending, approved, rejected) and a “why” panel that highlights sensitive-word hits; users can report content, and the interface must show the resulting disposition and any penalties. Front-desk Operators create bookings in 15-minute time slots, add order notes and attachments (for example, a signed waiver), reschedule or cancel, and verify attendance by scanning a printed QR code on a receipt or membership card; the order timeline should visually reflect state changes from reserved to confirmed to completed. Employees submit leave requests using configurable forms with attachment upload, see SLA countdowns, and withdraw or correct a request before final approval; Managers and HR Approvers receive queued approvals with conditional routing based on role, org unit, leave type, and duration, along with overdue reminders displayed in-app.

The backend uses Spring Boot to implement both server-rendered pages and decoupled REST-style endpoints consumed by the Thymeleaf screens and by local-network devices; MySQL persists all operational data, including versioned content (retain up to 20 versions per item with one-click rollback), immutable audit logs, and booking state transitions. Content moderation must be fully offline: pre-screening is rule-based using a locally maintained sensitive-word and policy tag dictionary, plus strict file validation (allow JPEG/PNG up to 20 MB, MP3 up to 50 MB, MP4 up to 200 MB; reject mismatched extensions via MIME sniffing) and checksum-based duplicate detection; three confirmed violations within 90 days trigger an automatic 30-day posting suspension, with an appeal note recorded. The booking engine must enforce concurrency-safe slot occupancy using database transactions and row-level locking; reservations expire after 10 minutes if unpaid/unconfirmed, and confirmed orders auto-close after a 30-minute no-show grace period unless manually overridden with a reason. “Payments” are recorded offline as tender entries (cash, check, or card-present captured by a LAN-connected terminal) with idempotent callbacks for device retries, revenue split defaults of 80/20 between merchant and platform configurable per location, and full/partial refunds allowed within 30 days with supervisor approval above $200.00; daily reconciliation produces exception queues (missing tender, duplicate callback, mismatch) and exportable reports. Open interfaces must support signed authentication (HMAC with rotating shared secrets), rate limiting at 60 requests per minute per user, and replay protection using nonces expiring in 5 minutes; webhooks are delivered only to whitelisted private IP ranges. Security is local-first: username/password login only (minimum 12 characters, complexity enforced, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes), session timeout after 30 minutes idle, PII masked in UI, and sensitive fields encrypted at rest; metrics and operational tracing are stored locally with alert rules such as payment failure rate above 3% in the last hour or negative slot inventory detected, and all audit and reconciliation records are retained for 7 years for compliance.
